hcd_data.statement_of_contribution: The property has value as an early-twentieth-century vernacular style two-storey commercial addition to a nineteenth-century residential structure. Through its construction date and typology, the property is associated with the District’s early residential development from the 1850s to the early 190

facade_description: Converted semi-detached house-form with commercial ground floor, now operating as Bristol Car and Truck Rental. Cross-gable roof over two storeys with party walls on both sides. Facade largely obscured by parked rental trucks and an enclosed seasonal patio structure; green-painted upper trim and brown horizontal siding are partially visible. Limited confidence in facade details due to obstruction.
